claimText: The Kodaikanal Solar Observatory is operated by the Indian Institute of Astrophysics, an autonomous institute of the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
evidenceExcerpt: Operated by the Indian Institute of Astrophysics (an autonomous institute of DST)
examUse: Statement-based question on the administrative control of Indian space and astronomical observatories.
distractorHooks: Operated by the Ministry of Earth Sciences

claimText: The Kodaikanal Solar Observatory is located in the Palani Range of hills in Southern India.
evidenceExcerpt: Located in the Palani Range of hills in Southern India.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching observatories to their specific hill ranges or geographical locations.

claimText: The Kodaikanal Solar Observatory is credited with the discovery of the Evershed Effect.
evidenceExcerpt: Known for the discovery of the Evershed Effect.
examUse: Statement-based question linking Indian observatories to their historical scientific discoveries.

claimText: The Evershed Effect refers to a nearly horizontal outflow of gases observed in the penumbrae of sunspots within the Sun's photospheric layer.
evidenceExcerpt: The Evershed Effect is a nearly horizontal outflow of gases observed in the penumbrae of sunspots in the photospheric layer (visible surface of the Sun).
examUse: Conceptual statement-based question on solar phenomena and sunspot characteristics.
distractorHooks: Associated with solar flares instead of sunspots
